{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01UhJLMqc4MoSRnGkNJV6z5y","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Dg1eF5BsYGebRdoRMJjNSJ","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'PYEOF'\n# Compare input and output sequences to find the difference\nwith open('/app/sequences.fasta') as f:\n    lines = f.read().strip().split('\\n')\n\ninput_seq = lines[1]\noutput_seq = lines[3]\n\nprint(f\"Input length: {len(input_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"Output length: {len(output_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"Length difference: {len(output_seq) - len(input_seq)}\")\n\n# Find the first position where they differ\nfor i in range(min(len(input_seq), len(output_seq))):\n    if input_seq[i] != output_seq[i]:\n        print(f\"\\nFirst difference at position {i}\")\n        print(f\"Input around pos {i}: ...{input_seq[max(0,i-20):i]}|{input_seq[i:i+40]}...\")\n        print(f\"Output around pos {i}: ...{output_seq[max(0,i-20):i]}|{output_seq[i:i+40]}...\")\n        break\n\n# Find from the end\nfor i in range(1, min(len(input_seq), len(output_seq))+1):\n    if input_seq[-i] != output_seq[-i]:\n        print(f\"\\nLast difference at position from end: {i}\")\n        input_end_pos = len(input_seq) - i\n        output_end_pos = len(output_seq) - i\n        print(f\"Input pos {input_end_pos}: ...{input_seq[input_end_pos-10:input_end_pos]}|{input_seq[input_end_pos:input_end_pos+20]}...\")\n        print(f\"Output pos {output_end_pos}: ...{output_seq[output_end_pos-10:output_end_pos]}|{output_seq[output_end_pos:output_end_pos+20]}...\")\n        break\n\n# The difference is an insertion in the output\ndiff_start = i  # position from first diff\n# input_seq[diff_start:] should match output_seq[diff_start + extra:]\n# Let's find what was inserted\nfirst_diff = None\nfor i in range(min(len(input_seq), len(output_seq))):\n    if input_seq[i] != output_seq[i]:\n        first_diff = i\n        break\n\nextra = len(output_seq) - len(input_seq)\n# Check if input[first_diff:] == output[first_diff + extra:]\nif input_seq[first_diff:] == output_seq[first_diff + extra:]:\n    print(f\"\\nThis is a pure INSERTION at position {first_diff}\")\n    inserted = output_seq[first_diff:first_diff + extra]\n    print(f\"Inserted sequence ({len(inserted)} bp): {inserted}\")\n    print(f\"\\nContext in input:\")\n    print(f\"  Before insertion: ...{input_seq[first_diff-30:first_diff]}\")\n    print(f\"  After insertion:  {input_seq[first_diff:first_diff+30]}...\")\nelse:\n    print(\"\\nNot a simple insertion, checking for substitution/complex change...\")\n    # Try to find what region changed\n    # Check if it's a substitution\n    for offset in range(-10, extra + 10):\n        if input_seq[first_diff:] == output_seq[first_diff + offset:]:\n            print(f\"Match found with offset {offset}\")\n            if offset > 0:\n                print(f\"Inserted: {output_seq[first_diff:first_diff+offset]}\")\n            break\nPYEOF","description":"Compare input and output sequences to find differences"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":615,"cache_read_input_tokens":32478,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":615,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":24,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d245ccc3-b879-4c29-908b-46491651a9b0","uuid":"ced2474a-4fdb-414d-9b76-f19225263fde"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01HdW79NaECYJupzM4uvJGL4","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_013zw311ZW7ZqsW1guswjTfw","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'PYEOF'\nimport subprocess\n\nwith open('/app/sequences.fasta') as f:\n    lines = f.read().strip().split('\\n')\ninput_seq = lines[1]\n\ndef rc(seq):\n    comp = {'a':'t','t':'a','g':'c','c':'g','A':'T','T':'A','G':'C','C':'G'}\n    return ''.join(comp[b] for b in reversed(seq))\n\ndef get_tm(seq):\n    result = subprocess.run(['oligotm', '-tp', '1', '-sc', '1', '-mv', '50', '-dv', '2', '-n', '0.8', '-d', '500', seq],\n                          capture_output=True, text=True)\n    return float(result.stdout.strip())\n\ninsertion = \"tagattagaagaagaattaagaagaagattaacagaaag\"\ninsert_pos = 215  # first position after insertion point\n\n# Forward primer annealing: input_seq[215:215+L]\n# Reverse primer annealing: rc(input_seq[215-L:215])\n\nprint(\"Forward primer annealing options:\")\nfor length in range(15, 46):\n    anneal = input_seq[insert_pos:insert_pos+length]\n    tm = get_tm(anneal)\n    if 58 <= tm <= 72:\n        print(f\"  len={length}, Tm={tm:.1f}, seq={anneal}\")\n\nprint(\"\\nReverse primer annealing options:\")\nfor length in range(15, 46):\n    anneal_region = input_seq[insert_pos-length:insert_pos]\n    anneal = rc(anneal_region)\n    tm = get_tm(anneal)\n    if 58 <= tm <= 72:\n        print(f\"  len={length}, Tm={tm:.1f}, seq={anneal}\")\n\n# Find best pair with Tm difference <= 5\nprint(\"\\nSearching for best pair (Tm diff <= 5):\")\nbest = None\nfor fwd_len in range(15, 46):\n    fwd_anneal = input_seq[insert_pos:insert_pos+fwd_len]\n    fwd_tm = get_tm(fwd_anneal)\n    if not (58 <= fwd_tm <= 72):\n        continue\n    for rev_len in range(15, 46):\n        rev_anneal = rc(input_seq[insert_pos-rev_len:insert_pos])\n        rev_tm = get_tm(rev_anneal)\n        if not (58 <= rev_tm <= 72):\n            continue\n        diff = abs(fwd_tm - rev_tm)\n        if diff <= 5:\n            if best is None or diff < best[0]:\n                best = (diff, fwd_len, rev_len, fwd_tm, rev_tm, fwd_anneal, rev_anneal)\n\nif best:\n    diff, fwd_len, rev_len, fwd_tm, rev_tm, fwd_anneal, rev_anneal = best\n    print(f\"  Best pair: fwd_len={fwd_len}, rev_len={rev_len}, fwd_Tm={fwd_tm:.2f}, rev_Tm={rev_tm:.2f}, diff={diff:.2f}\")\n    \n    # Full forward primer = insertion + annealing\n    fwd_primer = insertion + fwd_anneal\n    rev_primer = rev_anneal\n    \n    print(f\"\\nFull forward primer ({len(fwd_primer)} bp):\")\n    print(f\"  {fwd_primer}\")\n    print(f\"  Annealing part ({len(fwd_anneal)} bp): {fwd_anneal}\")\n    print(f\"  Annealing Tm: {fwd_tm:.2f}\")\n    \n    print(f\"\\nReverse primer ({len(rev_primer)} bp):\")\n    print(f\"  {rev_primer}\")\n    print(f\"  Annealing Tm: {rev_tm:.2f}\")\nPYEOF","description":"Find optimal primer lengths using oligotm","timeout":60000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1250,"cache_read_input_tokens":33093,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1250,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d245ccc3-b879-4c29-908b-46491651a9b0","uuid":"3aea6ce5-172a-4032-8d66-e8ddd958518e"}

python3\n\"\"\"\nTerminal-Bench Claude Code Hooks (unified script).\n\nHandles all three hook events via stdin payload inspection:\n- PreToolUse: tracks state, always allows\n- PostToolUse: tracks errors, verification results, loop detection\n- Stop: blocks exit if verification hasn't been attempted\n\nProtocol:\n  Input: JSON via stdin\n  Output: JSON via stdout wrapped in {\"hookSpecificOutput\": {...}}\n\nState persisted in /tmp/.tbench_state.json across invocations.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nSTATE_FILE = Path(\"/tmp/.tbench_state.json\")\n\n# Patterns that indicate the agent is running verification tests\nVERIFICATION_PATTERNS = [\n    \"tests/test.sh\",\n    \"tests/test_\",\n    \"pytest tests/\",\n    \"pytest -v\",\n    \"python -m pytest\",\n    \"python3 -m pytest\",\n    \"make test\",\n    \"cargo test\",\n    \"npm test\",\n    \"go test\",\n    \"bash test\",\n    \"./test.sh\",\n    \"ctest\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate test success\nSUCCESS_PATTERNS = [\n    \"passed\",\n    \" ok\",\n    \"0 failed\",\n    \"tests passed\",\n    \"all tests\",\n    \"PASS\",\n    \"OK (\",\n    \"0 errors\",\n    \"Build succeeded\",\n    \"All checks passed\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate errors in command output\nERROR_PATTERNS = [\n    \"error:\",\n    \"Error:\",\n    \"ERROR:\",\n    \"traceback\",\n    \"Traceback\",\n    \"FAILED\",\n    \"not found\",\n    \"No such file\",\n    \"permission denied\",\n    \"Permission denied\",\n    \"command not found\",\n    \"ModuleNotFoundError\",\n    \"ImportError\",\n    \"SyntaxError\",\n    \"NameError\",\n    \"TypeError\",\n    \"ValueError\",\n    \"FileNotFoundError\",\n    \"ConnectionRefusedError\",\n    \"OSError\",\n    \"RuntimeError\",\n    \"segmentation fault\",\n    \"Segmentation fault\",\n    \"core dumped\",\n    \"killed\",\n    \"Killed\",\n    \"Critical error:\",\n    \"AssertionError\",\n]\n\n# Common build artifacts that should be cleaned up\nBUILD_ARTIFACT_PATTERNS = [\n    \"a.out\",\n    \"*.o\",\n    \"*.obj\",\n    \"*.exe\",\n    \"*.dll\",\n    \"*.so\",\n    \"*.dylib\",\n    \"*.pyc\",\n    \"__pycache__\",\n    \".DS_Store\",\n    \"*.swp\",\n    \"*.swo\",\n    \"core\",\n    \"*.core\",\n]\n\n# Deliverable patterns - files that should NOT be cleaned\nDELIVERABLE_PATTERNS = [\n    \"main.py.c\",\n    \"main.c.py\",\n    \"move.txt\",\n    \"regex.txt\",\n    \"response.txt\",\n    \"solution.py\",\n    \"answer.txt\",\n    \"output.json\",\n    \"result.csv\",\n]\n\n\ndef load_state() -> dict:\n    if STATE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text())\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n            pass\n    return {\n        \"start_time\": time.time(),\n        \"turn_count\": 0,\n        \"file_edits\": {},\n        \"verification_attempted\": False,\n        \"verification_passed\": False,\n        \"consecutive_failures\": 0,\n        \"last_error_sig\": \"\",\n        \"stop_block_count\": 0,\n    }\n\n\ndef save_state(state: dict) -> None:\n    try:\n        STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state))\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n\n\ndef emit(payload: dict) -> None:\n    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout)\n\n\ndef emit_allow(reason: str = \"\") -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\",\n            \"permissionDecision\": \"allow\",\n            \"permissionDecisionReason\": reason,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef emit_notification(event: str, message: str) -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": event,\n            \"notification\": message,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef is_verification_command(cmd: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a command is running verification tests.\"\"\"\n    return any(pattern in cmd for pattern in VERIFICATION_PATTERNS)\n\n\ndef detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n        return \"PostToolUse\"\n    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n        return \"PreToolUse\"\n    return \"Stop\"\n\n\ndef handle_pretool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n    state[\"turn_count\"] = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0) + 1\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_input = data.get(\"tool_input\", {})\n\n    # Track file edits for loop detection\n    if tool_name in (\"Edit\", \"Write\"):\n        filepath = tool_input.get(\"file_path\", \"unknown\")\n        state[\"file_edits\"][filepath] = state[\"file_edits\"].get(filepath, 0) + 1\n\n    # Track verification attempts\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        cmd = tool_input.get(\"command\", \"\")\n        if is_verification_command(cmd):\n            if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n                # Reset stop counter when entering verification phase\n                state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n\n\ndef handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", \"\"))\n\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        # Detect errors\n        has_error = any(err in tool_output for err in ERROR_PATTERNS)\n\n        if has_error:\n            sig = tool_output[:200]\n            if sig == state.get(\"last_error_sig\", \"\"):\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) + 1\n            else:\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 1\n                state[\"last_error_sig\"] = sig\n        else:\n            state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 0\n\n        # Detect verification success\n        if state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n            if any(kw in tool_output for kw in SUCCESS_PATTERNS):\n                state[\"verification_passed\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n\n    # Emit warnings for loops\n    notifications = []\n\n    for filepath, count in state.get(\"file_edits\", {}).items():\n        if count >= 4:\n            notifications.append(\n                f\"[tbench] WARNING: {filepath} edited {count} times. \"\n                \"STOP editing this file. Re-read the requirements and try \"\n                \"a fundamentally different approach.\"\n            )\n\n    if state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) >= 3:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: {state['consecutive_failures']} consecutive failures \"\n            \"with the same error. Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n        )\n\n    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. Fix only what fails.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 40 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Run verification NOW to check progress. \"\n            \"Early feedback prevents wasted turns.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 150:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. You are running low on turns. \"\n            \"Wrap up — ensure tests pass and stop.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 120 and not state.get(\"verification_passed\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Tests not yet passing. \"\n            \"Simplify your approach. A minimal passing solution beats a failed ambitious one.\"\n        )\n\n    if notifications:\n        emit_notification(\"PostToolUse\", \" | \".join(notifications))\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef handle_stop(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        # After 3 blocks, let it go (avoid infinite loop)\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 3:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] BLOCKED: You have NOT run the test suite. \"\n            \"You MUST verify before stopping. Run: \"\n            \"cd /app && bash tests/test.sh \"\n            \"OR: cd /app && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v\"\n        )\n    elif not state.get(\"verification_passed\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 2:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] Tests were run but appear to have failed. \"\n            \"Review the test output, fix issues, and re-run verification. \"\n            \"If you believe tests actually passed, you may stop.\"\n        )\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    try:\n        data = json.load(sys.stdin)\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, EOFError):\n        data = {}\n\n    hook_type = detect_hook_type(data)\n\n    if hook_type == \"PreToolUse\":\n        handle_pretool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"PostToolUse\":\n        handle_posttool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"Stop\":\n        handle_stop(data)\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n","numLines":336,"startLine":1,"totalLines":336}}}

45))\nchecks.append((\"Rev anneal length 15-45\", 15 <= len(rev_anneal) <= 45))\nchecks.append((\"Fwd Tm 58-72\", 58 <= fwd_tm <= 72))\nchecks.append((\"Rev Tm 58-72\", 58 <= rev_tm <=
